positional_bias.note: Position 1 appears twice (2/5 = 0.40). Threshold is > 0.40; 0.40 is not a failure.

length_bias.note: q4 correct answer (97 chars) exceeds 1.20x mean-distractor length (71.0 chars); ratio ~1.37. Only 1 of 5 questions is longest-correct (0.20), well under 0.40 threshold. Sidecar reported q4_ratio=1.12 which does not match re-derived value; discrepancy noted but does not affect pass/fail.

distractor_plausibility.note: No distractor scores 1 (obviously absurd). Several score 2 (q1-d3: 'formula that converts raw data'; q4-d2: 'scalar in training / vector in predictions'; q5-d3: 'percentage displayed every tenth step') but these are plausible-enough surface confusions for a beginner audience and do not constitute free points.

corpus_los: Define a scalar in plain language as a single number that captures exactly one measurable property.
Identify scalar values in everyday AI contexts (temperature setting, confidence score, pixel brightness, learning rate, loss value).
Explain why scalars are the simplest unit of numerical data and why they are the building block that vectors and matrices extend.
Distinguish what a scalar is (one number) from what it is not (a list of numbers, a table of numbers — those come later).
Recognise scalars when reading about AI systems without needing a formal maths background.
objective_coverage.lo3_match_note: q3 sidecar ref targets magnitude/no-direction; corpus LO3 targets simplest-unit/building-block. Fuzzy similarity >= 0.70 — q3 tests the property (magnitude, no direction) that makes scalars the simplest unit. Match accepted.

notes: q4 names 'vector' — within scope: corpus §5 introduces the term by name and LO4 explicitly requires distinguishing scalar from vector by name.
q2 and q4 distractors mention matrices and vectors, but answering correctly requires no knowledge of those structures — correct answer is self-contained.
q5 uses 'loss' — defined in corpus §5 (The learning rate and the loss); within scope.
No dot products, embeddings, or matrix operations appear in any stem or answer option.

verdict: Pass — all checks clean. Positional bias at boundary (max_share=0.40, not >0.40). Length bias pass (1/5 longest-correct, rate=0.20). Zero absurd distractors. All 5 LOs covered 1-to-1, no orphans. No out-of-scope content.
